questions.question:
Throughout the excerpt, Sal watches the Winterbottom family interact and then reflects on what matters, ‘in the course of a lifetime.’

According to the last paragraph, how does watching the Winterbottom family help Sal understand the difference between things that matter and things that don't matter in life? Use evidence from the text to support your response.

responses.content: Understand why you should not be like that with her family

Watching the Winterbotton family helps Sal figure out what’s important in life. She says, “I did not think cheerleading tryouts would matter, but I was not so sure about yelling at your mother,” and”I was certain, however, that if your mother left, it would be something that mattered in the whole long course of your lifetime.” “Seeing the family problems make her think about her own life.”

questions.question:

responses.content: Munroe uses causes and effect and step by step to show how the problems started and how they progressed. This shows a chain reaction of how the interconnected systems hurt each other.
